sn.s: Jedi Sproch het ihri eigene Sprüch.
sn.e: Every language has its own sayings.

sn.s: Wer sie verstaht, verstaht au d'Lüt besser.
sn.e: Whoever understands them understands the people better too.

sn.s: "Es hät, solang's hät" heisst: solang's da isch, gits.
sn.e: "There is, as long as there is" means: while it lasts, you can have it.

sn.s: Mer ghört das oft am Buffet oder im Lade.
sn.e: You hear that often at a buffet or in a shop.

sn.s: "Das gaht uf kei Chuehuut" heisst, öppis isch unglaublich.
sn.e: "That doesn't fit on a cowhide" means something is outrageous.

sn.s: "Es Brötli verdiene" heisst, sis Gäld verdiene.
sn.e: "To earn a little bread" means to earn your living.

sn.s: Vili Sprüch händ mit Ässe oder Tier z'tue.
sn.e: Many sayings have to do with food or animals.

sn.s: Das verzellt öppis über s'Läbe vo früecher.
sn.e: That tells you something about life in the old days.

sn.s: Am Aafang han ich die Sprüch wörtli gnoo.
sn.e: At the start I took these sayings literally.

sn.s: Das het zu mängem Lache gfüehrt.
sn.e: That led to many a laugh.

sn.s: Jetz verstah ich de Witz dehinder.
sn.e: Now I understand the joke behind them.

sn.s: Und mängisch bruuch ich sälber eine.
sn.e: And sometimes I use one myself.

sn.s: D'Lüt staune und freued sich, wenn ich das mache.
sn.e: People are amazed and pleased when I do that.

sn.s: E Sproch wohnt nöd nu i de Wörter, au i de Sprüch.
sn.e: A language lives not only in the words, but in the sayings too.
